description: Fix three CLI UX issues: status output floods the terminal with 76 disabled tool names; audit --since gives a 'broaden your search' hint when no audit data exists at all; plugin install leaks npm noise and gives no next-steps after a successful install.

userStories.title: Remove disabled tools list from status output
userStories.description: The status command appends a 'Disabled Browser Tools' list of all 76 disabled tool names after the 14-line summary, making the total output 96 lines. Remove this block — the tool counts are already shown in the summary. Users who want the full list can run opentabs tool list.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs status output does not contain a 'Disabled Browser Tools' section
opentabs status output still shows 'Browser tools' and 'Plugin tools' numeric counts in the summary
opentabs status --json output still includes the browserToolNames array (this is used programmatically)

userStories.title: Fix misleading audit empty-state messages
userStories.description: When audit --since 1h (or any filter) is used on a fresh install with no audit data, the message says 'No entries match the current filters. Try broadening the search.' This implies data exists but was filtered out. Change both the file-path and server-path empty-state messages to not imply data exists when there is no audit history at all.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs audit --since 1h with no audit data returns 'No audit entries found. Invoke tools through an MCP client to generate audit entries.' (same as the no-filter message)
opentabs audit --plugin nonexistent with no audit data returns the same 'No audit entries found' message
opentabs audit (no filters) with no data still returns the original 'No audit entries found. Invoke tools through an MCP client to generate audit entries.' message
The --file path (handleAuditFromFile) also uses the same consistent message when file exists but entries are empty after filtering

userStories.title: Suppress npm install noise and add next-steps after plugin install
userStories.description: opentabs plugin install slack outputs raw npm messages ('added 4 packages in 736ms', '1 package is looking for funding...') because it uses spawnInherit with stdio: inherit. After a successful install, there are no next-steps hints. Fix: pass --loglevel=error --fund=false to suppress npm informational output. Add a next-steps hint after success that tells the user to restart the server if it's running.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin install slack output does not contain npm funding notices ('package is looking for funding')
opentabs plugin install slack output does not contain 'added N packages in Xms'
If npm install fails, npm error output is still visible (loglevel=error means errors still print)

After 'Successfully installed @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack.', the output includes a hint: if server is running (notifyServer succeeds), show 'Plugin loaded — run opentabs plugin list to see it.' If server is not running, show 'Start the server with: opentabs start'
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts

Context:
The install handler at line 193 uses spawnInherit which directly inherits stdio, forwarding all npm output:
```typescript
const exitCode = await spawnInherit('npm', ['install', '-g', pkg]);
```
spawnInherit is defined at lines 88-105 using stdio: 'inherit'. The npm output includes 'added N packages in Xms' (informational) and '1 package is looking for funding...' (npm funding notice).

Fix 1 — suppress npm noise:
Change the npm install call to add --loglevel=error and --fund=false flags:
```typescript
const exitCode = await spawnInherit('npm', ['install', '-g', pkg, '--loglevel=error', '--fund=false']);
```
This suppresses informational messages while still showing errors. The same change should be applied to the npm uninstall call at line 275:
```typescript
const exitCode = await spawnInherit('npm', ['uninstall', '-g', pkg]);
```
Change to:
```typescript
const exitCode = await spawnInherit('npm', ['uninstall', '-g', pkg, '--loglevel=error']);
```

Fix 2 — next-steps after install:
The current flow after install (lines 200-202):
```typescript
console.log(pc.green(`Successfully installed ${pkg}.`));
await warnIfNotPlugin(pkg);
await notifyServer(options);
```
notifyServer already prints either 'Server notified.' (success) or 'Server not running — changes will apply on next start.' (not running). That's the existing behavior for notifyServer when warnIfNotRunning is passed.

Look at how notifyServer is called for install — it's called as `await notifyServer(options)` without `warnIfNotRunning: true`. Check if the install path passes warnIfNotRunning. If not, the server-not-running path is silent.

Change the notifyServer call to:
```typescript
await notifyServer({ ...options, warnIfNotRunning: true, successMessage: pc.dim('Plugin loaded — run opentabs plugin list to see it.') });
```
This uses the existing notifyServer infrastructure:
- If server is running: prints the custom successMessage
- If server not running: prints 'Server not running — changes will apply on next start.'

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the spawnInherit function itself — only the call sites in the install/remove handlers
- Do NOT change the 'Resolving plugin...' and 'Installing ...' messages
- Do NOT change plugin remove behavior beyond the --loglevel=error flag
